patterns_recognized: Home-favorite overconfidence: yesterday's worst losses (SEA@CHW 74%, NYM@ARI 68%) were both high-model-confidence home favorites Vegas wasn't backing — model confidence >65% with market divergence is a persistent red flag.

Rookie/limited-start SP blindspot confirmed: NYY@MIL GOLD loss to Misiorowski (small-sample starter) exactly matches heuristic #3; today Payton Tolle (BOS, only 3 GS, 2.04 ERA) is the equivalent risk in TB@BOS.

Stage 1 / Stage 2 bullpen-carry pattern: PIT@SF shows a 26.9pp F5/full gap (SF F5=57.8% but SF full only 30.9%) — the 69.1% model confidence entirely depends on a late-inning PIT bullpen thesis that cannot be verified without rest data.

LAA @ TOR.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: edge +19.3pp exceeds the 15pp upper band. TOR at 67.4% raw; calibrated ~57% per the [0.6,0.7] bucket. Cease pitched yesterday (TOR 2-0 win), so today's SP matchup changes with Yesavage — a newer arm with limited MLB data. No structural edge overrides the band filter today.

OAK @ BAL.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: edge -26.1pp is deeply negative and far outside any actionable band. Yesterday BAL was picked and lost 4-3 to OAK. Today model flips to OAK at 57% but with no positive signals and a Vegas consensus shortfall of 26pp, this is noise. Civale vs Baz is a balanced SP matchup.

HOU @ CIN.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: no fair_prob returned (fetched_capped) so edge is unverifiable and stake_mult=0. CIN F5 dominance is very high (f5_prob=0.853 for CIN home), meaning HOU's 52.5% full-game pick is a pure bullpen-carry thesis. Arrighetti vs Burns today; Chase Burns has been dominant. Yesterday HOU won 10-0 but under different SP conditions.

WSH @ MIA.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: fair_prob=0.174 is far below the 0.42 floor, and edge +37.6pp is an extreme outlier. Yesterday MIA was picked and lost 2-3 in a low-conviction game. Lovelady (WSH opener) vs Junk (MIA) is a low-confidence SP matchup. The fair_prob filter is the correct gate.

COL @ PHI.reasoning: Downgrading GOLD→SKIP. Edge +15.92pp clips the 15pp upper band limit, fair_prob=0.350 is below the 0.42 floor (Vegas has PHI at 65%), and confidence_downgrade=True is pipeline-flagged. Aaron Nola (PHI) is a far tougher matchup than Luzardo was yesterday when COL won 9-7. Kyle Freeland struggles away from Coors. COL's model prob is barely 50.9% with three band/floor filters tripped.

MIN @ CLE.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: bullpen_fatigue=True and confidence_downgrade=True. CLE's F5 probability is only 40.9% (f5_prob=0.4093) while CLE full-game reads 57.2% — a 16.3pp Stage 1/2 gap. The pick depends on CLE's taxed bullpen producing a late comeback against Ryan (MIN). Ryan vs Bibee is a solid SP matchup where early innings likely favor MIN.

CHC @ TEX.reasoning: Confirming GOLD. Jack Leiter (TEX) is 5.40 ERA, 1.36 WHIP, 7 HR in 38.1 IP this season — legitimately exploitable. Edward Cabrera (CHC) provides a real xERA advantage (F1_xera_gap=0.80) and F2_xwoba_gap=0.025 fires independently. Vegas backs CHC at 55.3% implied, so we go WITH the market direction. Stage 1/2 gap=0.9pp, minimal. Stake_mult=0 per band rule on -4.41pp edge stands; direction is sound.

DET @ KC.reasoning: Downgrading GOLD→SKIP. acute_roster=True flags a live DET lineup concern that would erase the modest F2_xwoba_gap=0.021 edge. confidence_downgrade=True already pipeline-flagged. Yesterday DET was GOLD-picked in this same series and lost 3-4. Wacha (KC) is a durable veteran. A coin-flip model prob (51.3%) with acute roster risk and a fresh series loss is not actionable.

SEA @ CWS.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied. Yesterday was the model's worst loss: CHW at 74% lost 12-8 to SEA. Today CHW at 56.8% — calibrated ~52%. Luis Castillo (SEA) starts today, a genuine ace, against Anthony Kay (CWS). SEA's 12-run outburst reveals real offensive strength the model under-priced. No override warranted; if anything, SEA direction has stronger recent backing.

NYY @ MIL.reasoning: Confirming GOLD. Cam Schlittler (NYY) has been dominant: 8 GS, 1.52 ERA, 0.87 WHIP, 5-1 record. The F1_xera_gap=1.68* asterisk likely flags Kyle Harrison (MIL) on a shorter sample — Schlittler's 8-start track record is sufficient to trust. Vegas at 56.2% slightly favors NYY, aligning with the model's 54.2%. Stage 1/2 gap=13.6pp is below the 15pp threshold. bullpen_fatigue noted but Schlittler's efficiency should limit bullpen exposure.

NYM @ ARI.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied. Yesterday ARI was picked at 68% and lost 1-3 — this is the exact home-favorite overconfidence pattern (Heuristic #1). Edge +17.6pp is outside the band. Calibrated ARI ~57% from 67.4% raw. Today Holmes (NYM) vs Kelly (ARI). The model's repeated 67%+ ARI confidence against a near-neutral Vegas line is the key warning pattern from yesterday.

STL @ SD.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied: no signals firing, tier remains SKIP. Dustin May (STL) vs Randy Vasquez (SD). Yesterday SD was picked and lost; model flips to STL at 55.4% with edge +12.9pp — within band but no tier elevation granted. Without active F-signals, SKIP is correct.

PIT @ SF.reasoning: Downgrading GOLD→SKIP. Stage 1/2 gap is 26.9pp: SF wins F5 at 57.8% (f5_prob=0.5781) but SF wins full game at only 30.9% — PIT's 69.1% model confidence entirely depends on a late-inning bullpen-carry thesis. Vegas prices this near 50/50 (fair_prob=0.503), creating a 19pp model-market gap that echoes yesterday's overconfidence pattern. Both starters are solid (Ashcraft 3.02 ERA, Roupp 3.18 ERA). Cannot confirm PIT bullpen rest.

ATL @ LAD.reasoning: SKIP correctly applied with maximum confidence: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO is the strongest pipeline filter, edge -9.49pp breaches the -8pp threshold (Heuristic #2), and bullpen_fatigue=True. Blake Snell (LAD) injury history makes SP comparisons unreliable. Yesterday LAD won 3-1 behind Sheehan; today's Snell matchup is a different risk profile. Hard veto stands unconditionally.

TB @ BOS.reasoning: Downgrading GOLD→SKIP. Payton Tolle (BOS) has only 3 MLB starts in 2026 (17.2 IP), triggering the <6-start SP-edge unreliability heuristic (#3). Critically, Tolle has been excellent (2.04 ERA, 0.74 WHIP, 11.7 K/9) — model likely underrates him, inflating TB's lineup edge. Yesterday TB lost 0-2 to BOS in the identical setup (underdog with lineup quality vs quality SP). No odds data (no_match) prevents edge verification. F3=911.8 is real but insufficient given compounding uncertainties.
